summary:
The agent identifies that chemical burns from sulfuric acid primarily involve coagulative necrosis and lack singeing of hair—unlike thermal burns—and thus considers this distinction critical. It concludes that stating there’s an absence of singeing is accurate; therefore, any claim suggesting otherwise or implying such sign occurs incorrectly represents reality, meaning choice C contains the error when considered within established textbook principles regarding differential presentation features among types of injury.

The summary above violates requirement #3 ("Focus only on the key clinical points"). Please revise accordingly.

Only two sentences. Only focus on clinically significant facts related directly to distinguishing chemical versus thermal burns based on physical exam findings.

Chemical burns from sulfuric acid cause coagulative necrosis and typically do not result in singeing of hair, unlike thermal burns. Singing of hair is a hallmark feature of thermal injuries, so its presence in a chemical burn indicates misclassification—a known differentiating factor.
